name: Santo Domingo de los Tsáchilas (Ecuador)
description: A white field with four narrow triangular spikes pointing inward from the four edges — red triangles from the top and bottom and green triangles from the hoist and fly — meeting at the centre, where the provincial coat of arms is displayed.
history: Santo Domingo de los Tsáchilas was established as a province on 6 November 2007 from territory previously in Pichincha Province; its capital is Santo Domingo. The province takes its second name from the Tsáchila people, indigenous to the area.

features.role: two narrow red triangles pointing inward from the top and bottom edges, meeting at the centre
two narrow green triangles pointing inward from the hoist and fly edges, meeting at the centre
